Sean Davis (06:22:36): > For folks working in cancer genetics/somatic mutation space, would love additional contributions to:https://github.com/seandavi/awesome-cancer-variant-resources

Ludwig Geistlinger (08:40:20) (in thread): > re: Variant Effect Prediction tools and databases. Would we want to addAlphaMissense+AlphaMissenseR? Can open a PR. - Attachment (Zenodo): Predictions for AlphaMissense > This repository provide AlphaMissense predictions. Please see the README for more details. For questions about AlphaMissense or the prediction Database please email mailto:alphamissense@google.com|alphamissense@google.com. - Attachment (Bioconductor): AlphaMissenseR

Kate Penrose (09:29:59): > @Kate Penrose has joined the channel

Sean Davis (10:45:21) (in thread): > Awesome!

Sean Davis (10:48:42) (in thread): > Thanks, all, for the feedback in realtime!

Sean Davis (10:49:58) (in thread): > The NIH folks reached out to let me know that this was not a planned event. I didn’t get more details nor did I ask for them.

Sean Davis (10:52:02) (in thread): > A positive result was the increased visibility and news coverage of some of these critical resources. Hopefully, it was a singular blip.

Robert Castelo (13:11:25) (in thread): > You may also want to add the Bioconductor packageGenomicScoresthat provides access to different types of genomic scores including conservation, minor allele frequencies or pathogeniticy, such as CADD or AlphaMissense scores (see section 7 of itsvignette). - Attachment (Bioconductor): GenomicScores > Provide infrastructure to store and access genomewide position-specific scores within R and Bioconductor.

Yanina Bellini Saibene (09:41:04): > rOpenSci March news!:envelope_with_arrow:#RStats:mega:rOpenSci Champions Program 2025 In Spanish: Apply before April 30th!:tada:New rOpenSci Champion(a|e)s Program cohort:rocket:Better documentation for R-universe!:handshake:rOpenSci Participation at NumFOCUS’ DISC Unconf 2025:video_camera:Community Call “Fomentando la Ciencia Abierta en América Latina - Programa de rOpenSci” Resources:spiral_calendar_pad:Coworking:world_map:New packages {pangoling}, {mbquartR}, {mapmetadata}, {geotargets}, {saperlipopette}:sparkles:3 use cases, of {osmextract} and {babelquarto}:memo:Blog posts:raised_back_of_hand:Calls for contributions:toolbox:Package development cornerhttps://ropensci.org/blog/2025/03/27/news-march-2025/ - Attachment (ropensci.org): rOpenSci News Digest, March 2025 > Call for

Claire Rioualen (09:56:10) (in thread): > Hi! Not sure about internal organisation, but if you’re interested in data management and deposition: > * In France there is a national repository for data deposition, and “thematic reference centres” that can help or orientate people depending on their research Focus > * In Europe there are also some useful resources provided or recomended by the ELIXIR network, such as theResearch Data Management community, a list ofdatabases for data deposition, or maybe theData Stewardship Wizardresources - Attachment (recherche.data.gouv.fr): Recherche Data Gouv > To share or open up research data, these need to be deposited in a trustworthy data repository that promotes the FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable,… - Attachment (elixir-europe.org): ELIXIR Deposition Databases for Biomolecular Data | ELIXIR > ELIXIR has compiled a list of resources that it recommends for the deposition of experimental data. The scientific community has a shared responsibility to ensure long-term data preservation and accessibility. The purpose of this Deposition Databases list is to provide guidance to those who formulate policy and working practices about the appropriate repositories for publishing open data in the life sciences. - Attachment (ds-wizard.org): Data Stewardship Wizard > Leading open-source platform for collaborative and living data management plans trusted worldwide from data management pioneers to international research institutes.

Lori Shepherd (15:21:24): > Bioconductor 3.21 is released! > Thanks to all developers and community members for contributing to the project! > Please see the full release announcement:https://bioconductor.org/news/bioc_3_21_release/ - Attachment (bioconductor.org): Bioconductor - Bioconductor 3.21 Released > The Bioconductor project aims to develop and share open source software for precise and repeatable analysis of biological data. We foster an inclusive and collaborative community of developers and data scientists.
